---
pagetitle: "Ran Wang | Academic Website"
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

::: {.hero-name role="heading" aria-level="1"}
Ran Wang
:::

::: {.hero-kicker}
Urban & Regional Economics · Scientific Mobility · Regional Innovation
:::

::: {.hero-summary}
I am an empirical researcher trained in economic geography. I study scientific mobility, knowledge networks, institutional platforms, and urban innovation using longitudinal data on scientists, institutions, publications, and cities.
:::

::: {.hero-actions}
[View Research](research.html){.btn .btn-hero-primary}
[Publications](publications.html){.btn .btn-hero-secondary}
[Selected Projects](projects.html){.btn .btn-hero-outline}
[Download CV](assets/cv/Ran_Wang_CV_Public.pdf){.btn .btn-hero-outline}
:::

::: {.photo-credit}
Photo: John Rodenn Castillo / Unsplash
:::

::: {.panel .panel-light}
## Research Positioning

My work connects individual scientific careers with the urban and regional systems in which knowledge production takes place. The main line of research examines how elite scientists move across institutions and cities, how prior knowledge-space connections are associated with destination choice, and how mobility and collaboration networks relate to urban innovation conditions.
:::

::: {.panel .panel-muted}
## Research Themes

::: {.theme-grid}
::: {.theme-card}
### Scientific Mobility and Career Trajectories

Longitudinal records of scientists, institutions, publications, and cities are used to describe career moves and intercity mobility networks.
:::

::: {.theme-card}
### Urban and Regional Innovation

The research links mobility, institutional platforms, collaboration networks, and city-level opportunities without treating observed associations as causal claims beyond the design.
:::

::: {.theme-card}
### Relational Data and Spatial Methods

Author-institution-city linkage, geocoding checks, network indicators, and event-level candidate sets support reproducible empirical work.
:::
:::
:::

::: {.panel .panel-dark}
## Selected Publications

**Accepted Article.** *Spatial Structure and Determinants of Top Global Biomedical Scientists' Mobility from an Urban Network Perspective*.

**Working Paper · In Revision.** *Pre-move Publication-Location Exposure and Destination Choice among Elite Biomedical Scientists*.

**Public Research Output.** *Author-Institution Linkage and Geocoding Feasibility Audit*. [View repository](https://github.com/Olivia-wr-research/author-institution-city-linkage)
:::

::: {.panel .panel-light}
## Selected Projects

::: {.project-list}
::: {.project-card}
[Flagship Research Programme]{.status-badge}

### Scientific Mobility, Knowledge Networks, and Urban Innovation

A research programme on elite scientists' career mobility, knowledge-space connections, collaboration networks, and urban innovation conditions.
:::

::: {.project-card}
[Collaborative Research Project]{.status-badge}

### Daily Nighttime Lights and Urban Resilience Trajectories

A collaborative project using high-frequency nighttime light data to describe changes and recovery trajectories in urban activity after external shocks.
:::

::: {.project-card}
[Ongoing Research]{.status-badge}

### Talent Mobility and Biomedical Innovation Systems in China and the United States

An ongoing comparative project on top scientist training, career mobility, research output, patent activity, industrial foundations, and knowledge translation.
:::
:::
:::

::: {.panel .panel-muted}
## Quick Links

::: {.quick-links}
[Research](research.html)
[Publications](publications.html)
[Projects](projects.html)
[CV](cv.html)
[Contact](contact.html)
:::
:::
